description: In Go, read the first compiler or vet message with package path and line before making speculative edits.
body: In Go, the toolchain already points at the real problem, so the first compiler or vet message with package path and line should guide the edit rather than a guess. Use when go vet ./... and the compiler's unused-variable/import errors or go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) reports an error or warning. Do not start editing before reading the diagnostic, and avoid changing code you have not confirmed is implicated. Actions: read the first actionable diagnostic and the owned frame, inspect that location, make the smallest fix, then rerun go test ./... -race on the changed package. Validation signals include the resolved diagnostic, a passing focused test, and no new warnings from go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race). Risk boundary: if the diagnostic exposes a deeper design fault, escalate rather than papering over it.
